segments.text: Why do these tiny pills [music] come in such massive plastic packets? It looks like a huge waste of plastic, but there's actually a genius reason behind this. Normally, you would think they
[music] could just shrink the packet down to perfectly fit that one tiny pen,
right? But hold on. If they make the packet small, how would you even know what you're taking? And that's [music]
exactly the point. The government legally requires companies to print a ton of information right onto the back.
We're talking warnings, ingredients, and expiry dates. So, the giant packet [music] isn't a waste of plastic. It's actually a design feature meant to keep you safe. For more design secrets,
